name: tsuru
description: Tsuru is the command line utility used by application developers, that will allow users to create, list, bind and manage apps

subcommands.name: app
subcommands.description: App commands

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Builds a tsuru app image respecting

name: -f
--files-only
options.description: Enables single file build into the root of the app's tree

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Creates a new app using the given name and platform

name: -t
--team
options.description: Team that will own the app

args.name: team
args.description: Pool where the app will be created

name: -p
--plan
options.description: Plan to be used by the app

args.name: plan
args.description: Plan to be used by the app

name: -g
--tag
options.description: Tag to be used by the app

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploys set of files and/or directories to tsuru server

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List information about deploys for an application

subcommands.name: rebuild
subcommands.description: Rebuild and deploy the last app image

name: --new-version
options.description: Creates a new version for the current deployment while preserving existing versions

name: --override-old-versions
options.description: Force replace all deployed versions by this new deploy

subcommands.name: rollback
subcommands.description: Deploys an existing image for an app

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Locks an existing image of an app

name: -d
--disable
options.description: Enables or disables the rollback on a specific image version

name: -i
--image
options.description: The image name for an app version

name: -r
--reason
options.description: The reason why the rollback has to be disabled

name: --new-version
options.description: Creates a new version for the current deployment while preserving existing versions

name: --override-old-versions
options.description: Force replace all deployed versions by this new deploy

name: -f
--files-only
options.description: Enables single file deployment into the root of the app's tree

name: -i
--image
options.description: The image to deploy in app

name: --new-version
options.description: Creates a new version for the current deployment while preserving existing versions

name: --override-old-versions
options.description: Force replace all deployed versions by this new deploy

subcommands.name: grant
subcommands.description: Allows a team to access an application

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Shows information about an app

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all apps

name: -s
--status
options.description: Filter applications by unit status. Accepts multiple values separated by commas. Possible values can be: building, created, starting, error, started, stopped, asleep

name: -t
--team
options.description: Team that will own the app

args.name: team
args.description: Pool where the app will be created

subcommands.name: log
subcommands.description: Shows the logs of an app

name: -s
--source
options.description: The log from the given source

name: -u
--unit
options.description: The log from the given unit

subcommands.name: metadata
subcommands.description: Manage metadata for an app

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Retrieves metadata for an application

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets metadata for an application

name: --no-restart
options.description: Do not restart the app after the metadata is set

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Unsets metadata for an application

name: --no-restart
options.description: Do not restart the app after the metadata is set

subcommands.name: quota
subcommands.description: Shows the quota of an app

subcommands.name: change
subcommands.description: Changes the limit of units that an app can have

subcommands.name: view
subcommands.description: Displays the current usage and limit of the given app

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes an app

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Restarts an application, or one of the processes of the application

subcommands.name: revoke
subcommands.description: Removes a team access to an application

subcommands.name: router
subcommands.description: Manage routers for an app

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds a router to an app

name: -o
--opts
options.description: Custom options sent directly to router implementation

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List routers for an app

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes a router from an app

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates a router for an app

name: -o
--opts
options.description: Custom options sent directly to router implementation

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Manage router versions for an app

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds a router version to an app

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes a router version from an app

subcommands.name: routes
subcommands.description: List all routers

subcommands.name: rebuild
subcommands.description: Rebuild routes for an application

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Runs a command inside an app

subcommands.name: shell
subcommands.description: Opens a remote shell inside unit, using the API server as a proxy

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Starts an app

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stops an application

subcommands.name: swap
subcommands.description: Swaps routing between two apps

name: -f
--force
options.description: Force Swap among apps with different number of units or different platform

subcommands.name: unlock
subcommands.description: Forces the removal of an application lock

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates an app

name: --cpu
options.description: CPU limit for app, this will override the plan cpu value. One cpu is equivalent to 1 vCPU/Core, fractional requests are allowed and the expression 0.1 is equivalent to the expression 100m

name: -i
--image-reset
options.description: Forces next deploy to build app image from scratch

name: --memory
options.description: Memory limit for app, this will override the plan memory value. You can express memory as a bytes integer or using one of these suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, K, Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki

name: --no-restart
options.description: Prevent tsuru from restarting the application

name: -p
--plan
options.description: Plan to be used by the app

args.name: plan
args.description: Plan to be used by the app

name: -t
--team
options.description: Team that will own the app

args.name: team
args.description: Pool where the app will be created

subcommands.name: env
subcommands.description: App environment variables

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get environment variables from an app

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set environment variables to an app

name: --no-restart
options.description: Don't restart the app after setting the env vars

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Unset environment variables from an app

name: --no-restart
options.description: Don't restart the app after setting the env vars

name: --no-restart
options.description: Don't restart the app after setting the env vars

subcommands.name: cluster
subcommands.description: Cluster commands

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a new cluster

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all clusters

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a cluster

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update a cluster

subcommands.name: certificate
subcommands.description: Certificate commands

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List App TLS certificates

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Creates or update a TLS certificate into the specific app

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Unset a TLS certificate from a specific app

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Login to tsuru server

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Logout from tsuru server
